name: 80_advisory_judge
description: The headline metric: does /retrieve surface EVERY fact needed to answer an advisory question well? Judged by a strong model.
changelog: v3: approve_pending step added (was v2 gap); assertions unchanged from v2 except distributor-deal wording loosened from 'declined due to thin margins and revenue concentration' to 'declined or evaluated and passed on' — matches extraction reality without weakening what's tested. Headcount probe added as non-graded informational.

steps.user_message: I'm Adam, founder of IIMAGINE — 7 people, desktop AI companion for small business owners. MRR $85k, runway 14 months.
steps.assistant_response: Solid base: 7-person team, $85k MRR, 14 months runway.

steps.user_message: I hate debt. Non-negotiable — we grow from revenue only.
Last year I declined a wholesale distribution deal with Baxter & Co because margins were thin and it would've been 60% of our revenue — too concentrated.
steps.assistant_response: Concentration plus thin margins — right call to pass.

steps.user_message: Our objective this year is 500 paying customers, up from about 300 now.
Sarah Chen, our CTO, says the engineering team is at full capacity — no slack for new product lines this quarter.
steps.assistant_response: Engineering at capacity per your CTO — worth remembering for roadmap asks.

steps.user_message: I personally want to keep weekends free. I'd rather grow slower than work seven days.
We're strong at product and engineering but honestly weak at sales — nobody on the team has real outbound experience.
steps.assistant_response: Product-strong, sales-weak. A hiring or partnership gap to watch.

steps.label: distributor-deal question pulls preference + precedent + capacity
steps.question: A national distributor wants to white-label our product and handle sales, taking a 55% revenue share. Should I take the deal?
required_facts: The owner previously declined a wholesale/distribution deal (Baxter & Co)
The margins on that deal were thin
The deal would have concentrated revenue (60%)
The business is weak at sales / lacks outbound experience
Current revenue or runway position ($85k MRR and/or 14 months runway)

steps.label: financing question pulls the zero-debt preference
steps.question: The bank offered us a $200k line of credit at good rates to accelerate hiring. Should we take it?
required_facts: The owner has a firm zero-debt preference (grow from revenue only)

steps.label: new-product question pulls capacity + objective + team size
steps.question: I'm considering launching a second product — an invoicing tool. Does it make sense to start now?
required_facts: Engineering is at full capacity with no slack for new product lines this quarter
This year's objective is reaching 500 paying customers

steps.label: workload question pulls the lifestyle preference
steps.question: A big prospect wants weekend onboarding support as a contract condition. Should I agree?
required_facts: The owner wants to keep weekends free
The owner prefers slower growth or would rather not work seven days

steps.label: [probe] headcount in context (non-graded)
steps.question: How big is our team right now?
steps._note: Informational only — tracks whether mid-salience operational figures make it through retrieval. Not a target metric.
